title: Reducing Hallucinations in LLMs via Factuality-Aware Preference Learning
problem_statement: Preference alignment methods like RLHF and DPO can reinforce hallucinations by rewarding fluency and confidence over factual correctness. This paper addresses the core technical problem of improving factuality in aligned LLMs without requiring auxiliary reward models, token-level annotations, or multi-stage training.

core_ideas.name: F-DPO (Factuality-aware Direct Preference Optimization)
core_ideas.mechanism: Uses binary factuality labels to (i) apply a label-flipping transformation that corrects misordered preference pairs so the chosen response is never less factual than the rejected one, and (ii) add a factuality-aware margin that emphasizes pairs with clear correctness differences, reducing to standard DPO when both responses share the same factuality.
evidence_phrases: F-DPO (Factuality-aware Direct Preference Optimization), a simple extension of DPO that uses only binary factuality labels
applies a label-flipping transformation that corrects misordered preference pairs
adds a factuality-aware margin that emphasizes pairs with clear correctness differences

evidence_refs.quote: F-DPO (Factuality-aware Direct Preference Optimization), a simple extension of DPO that uses only binary factuality labels

experiment_signals.setting: Across seven open-weight LLMs (1B-14B) with factuality-aware preference data constructed by augmenting DPO pairs with binary factuality indicators and synthetic hallucinated variants
experiment_signals.claim: F-DPO consistently improves factuality and reduces hallucination rates relative to both base models and standard DPO, and generalizes to out-of-distribution benchmarks
evidence_phrases: F-DPO consistently improves factuality and reduces hallucination rates relative to both base models and standard DPO
On Qwen3-8B, F-DPO reduces hallucination rates by five times (from 0.424 to 0.084) while improving factuality scores by 50 percent (from 5.26 to 7.90)
on TruthfulQA, Qwen2.5-14B achieves plus 17 percent MC1 accuracy (0.500 to 0.585) and plus 49 percent MC2 accuracy (0.357 to 0.531)

evidence_refs.quote: F-DPO consistently improves factuality and reduces hallucination rates relative to both base models and standard DPO

title: Outcome-based Exploration for LLM Reasoning
problem_statement: Outcome-based RL for LLM reasoning improves accuracy but causes a systematic loss in generation diversity, which undermines real-world performance and test-time scaling. This collapse occurs even on the training set and propagates from solved to unsolved problems.

explicit_limitations.name: Diversity collapse from outcome-based RL
explicit_limitations.description: Outcome-based RL rewards only final answer correctness, leading to reduced generation diversity that harms test-time scaling.
evidence_phrases: Outcome-based RL, which rewards policies solely for the correctness of the final answer, yields substantial accuracy gains but also induces a systematic loss in generation diversity.

evidence_refs.quote: Outcome-based RL, which rewards policies solely for the correctness of the final answer, yields substantial accuracy gains but also induces a systematic loss in generation diversity.

explicit_limitations.name: Diversity degradation propagates
explicit_limitations.description: Reduced diversity on solved problems transfers to unsolved problems, worsening overall performance.
evidence_phrases: a transfer of diversity degradation, where reduced diversity on solved problems propagates to unsolved ones

evidence_refs.quote: a transfer of diversity degradation, where reduced diversity on solved problems propagates to unsolved ones

core_ideas.name: Outcome-based exploration
core_ideas.mechanism: Assign exploration bonuses based on final outcomes to encourage diverse answers and mitigate collapse.
evidence_phrases: Motivated by these insights, we propose outcome-based exploration, which assigns exploration bonuses according to final outcomes.

evidence_refs.quote: Motivated by these insights, we propose outcome-based exploration, which assigns exploration bonuses according to final outcomes.

core_ideas.name: Historical exploration

evidence_refs.quote: historical exploration, which encourages rarely observed answers via UCB-style bonuses

evidence_phrases: batch exploration, which penalizes within-batch repetition to promote test-time diversity

evidence_refs.quote: batch exploration, which penalizes within-batch repetition to promote test-time diversity

core_ideas.name: Outcome-based bandits formalization
core_ideas.mechanism: New model of outcome-based bandits formalizes the benefit of outcome-based exploration.
evidence_phrases: we formalize the benefit of outcome-based exploration through a new model of outcome-based bandits

evidence_refs.quote: we formalize the benefit of outcome-based exploration through a new model of outcome-based bandits

experiment_signals.setting: Standard competition math with Llama and Qwen models
experiment_signals.claim: Both historical and batch exploration improve accuracy while mitigating diversity collapse.
evidence_phrases: Experiments on standard competition math with Llama and Qwen models demonstrate that both methods improve accuracy while mitigating diversity collapse.

evidence_refs.quote: Experiments on standard competition math with Llama and Qwen models demonstrate that both methods improve accuracy while mitigating diversity collapse.

title: ReCAD: Reinforcement Learning Enhanced Parametric CAD Model Generation with Vision-Language Models
problem_statement: The core technical problem is generating precise parametric computer-aided design (CAD) models from multimodal inputs (e.g., text, images) while leveraging the generative priors of pretrained large models. title: On the Non-decoupling of Supervised Fine-tuning and Reinforcement Learning in Post-training

title: From Memorization to Creativity: LLM as a Designer of Novel Neural-Architectures
problem_statement: Large language models (LLMs) excel in program synthesis but their ability to autonomously navigate neural architecture design—balancing syntactic reliability, performance, and structural novelty—remains underexplored. This work addresses this by placing a code-oriented LLM within a closed-loop synthesis framework that iteratively generates, validates, and refines convolutional network architectures using execution feedback.

core_ideas.name: Closed-loop synthesis with execution feedback
core_ideas.mechanism: A code-oriented LLM synthesizes PyTorch convolutional networks that are validated, evaluated via low-fidelity performance signals (single-epoch accuracy), and filtered using a MinHash-Jaccard criterion to prevent structural redundancy; high-performing, novel architectures are converted into prompt-code pairs for iterative fine-tuning via parameter-efficient LoRA adaptation.
target_limitations: balancing syntactic reliability, performance, and novelty
